Characteristics of Buzz
Module 3
Buzz is…
• Partially word of mouth…
• Getting people talking about you
• Think of a hot new movie that’s coming out
• Everyone starts talking about it
• It happens with music, trends, clothes, etc…
Buzz is…
• You want to get people to talk about you…
• It’s not super formulaic
• It’s going to depend on:
• Where you're at in your business
• What you’re launching
• The assets you have in your business
• A little bit of buzz can really move the needle for
you
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

© Internet Alchemy, Inc. All rights reserved. 	2
Characteristics of Buzz
• Buzz travels most easily through channels that
have trust and authenticity
• It can’t be a pure sales message either
• Share great content, create great value before
you ask for the sale
• Helps to create buzz
Characteristics of Buzz
• Multiple modalities and media
• Word of mouth
• Advertising
• Social
• Every market is different
• A little mystery goes a long way
How to Create Buzz
• Be authentic
• Create value
• Drop hints and leave a trail of breadcrumbs
• Start talking about it long in advance
• Have a contest around the idea you’re
launching
• Makes people feel like insiders when they
notice the breadcrumbs
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 4 --

© Internet Alchemy, Inc. All rights reserved. 	3
How to Create Buzz
• Engage raving fans and champions
• The people that are closest to you want to help
spread your message
• Engage influencers (mavens and connectors)
• Develop relationships with them
• See if they’ll mention you, interview you, etc.
• Use the Pre-Prelaunch
Potential assets that could help you
• Your lists (prospect, client)
• Blog
• Social media presence
• Forums
Potential assets that could help you
• Relationships
• (affiliates, top blogs, mavens and connectors)
• Speaking
• (events,, etc.)
• People at events are connected and tend to
spread the word
• Can be webinars, podcasts, etc.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

© Internet Alchemy, Inc. All rights reserved. 	4
Other ways to Build Buzz
• Run contests
• Go live
• Notify JV Partners (if applicable)
• Do interviews and speaking engagements
• Gather case studies from existing students
• Post a survey or quiz on your blog or website
• Tease social media
Building Buzz
• Think about how you can do this in every launch
• Get creative
